meta.version: GitHub Pages Static SPA v3 high-score polish
meta.routes: home, health-check, diagnosis, strategy-profile, monitor, evidence, report, pricing, architecture, risk, docs, tour
meta.runtime_note: No Python backend is required after deployment.

templates.name: Trend Pullback With Volatility Filter
templates.description: A trend-following template that waits for pullbacks inside an established trend and filters out low-volatility noise.
templates.recommended_for: Swing traders who want fewer noisy entries
templates.key_modules: trend filter; pullback entry; ATR stop; time-based exit
templates.main_risks: late entries; whipsaw in sideways regimes

templates.name: Breakout With Retest Confirmation
templates.description: A breakout template that requires price expansion and a retest confirmation before entering.
templates.recommended_for: Momentum traders who want fewer false breakouts
templates.key_modules: range detection; breakout trigger; retest check; volume filter; stop loss
templates.main_risks: missed trades; false breakouts during news spikes

templates.name: Mean Reversion With Regime Filter
templates.description: A mean-reversion template that only trades when the market is not strongly trending.
templates.key_modules: z-score entry; trend-regime filter; max holding period; stop loss

templates.name: Alert-First Forward Test Monitor
templates.description: A monitoring template for users who already have TradingView alerts and want to evaluate live alert quality before execution.
templates.key_modules: webhook logging; signal outcome tracking; alert latency check; report generation
templates.main_risks: requires enough live alerts to become statistically useful

templates.name: Research-Inspired ETF Rotation
templates.description: A rotation template that ranks ETF candidates using momentum and risk filters.
templates.recommended_for: Users who want a transparent research-inspired allocation strategy
templates.key_modules: cross-sectional rank; volatility scaling; rebalance schedule; drawdown control

templates.name: Paid Script Buyer Check
templates.description: A due-diligence template for checking whether a purchased script or indicator has credible claims and live behavior.
templates.recommended_for: Users evaluating paid indicators or invite-only scripts
templates.key_modules: claim checklist; non-repaint warning; alert log review; forward-test requirement
templates.main_risks: limited if source code is unavailable

templates.name: Webhook Reliability Monitor
templates.description: A data-product template that treats alert messages as event data and tracks delivery frequency outcomes and missing fields.
templates.key_modules: webhook payload parser; event schema; missing-field check; outcome attribution

evidence.short_evidence: d-bars toggle governs what the script computes. In Live mode the centerline, momentum, bands and signals update intrabar on the developing bar for the fastest response, with the current bar able to change until it closes. In Confirmed mode everything is locked to closed bars only, so signals do not repaint and print on the bar that closes the move.

evidence.short_evidence: TradingView documentation explains repainting as a case where historical and realtime calculations or plots behave differently.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports the claim that repainting is an official platform-level technical issue rather than an invented pain point

evidence.short_evidence: TradingView documentation explains that alerts can send HTTP POST requests to a configured webhook URL.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports PineGuard's webhook-based alert collection and forward-test monitoring design

evidence.short_evidence: TradingView explains that repainting occurs when historical and realtime calculations or plots behave differently.
evidence.why_relevant: Shows that historical/live mismatch is an official Pine Script reliability issue

evidence.short_evidence: TradingView documents request.security and higher-timeframe data behavior that can create misleading historical signals if used incorrectly.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports PineGuard's need to flag lookahead and higher-timeframe reliability risks

evidence.short_evidence: TradingView strategy documentation discusses broker-emulator behavior orders commissions slippage and strategy-testing assumptions.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports the claim that backtest realism depends on execution assumptions rather than raw chart signals alone

evidence.short_evidence: TradingView supports webhook alerts that send HTTP POST requests to external URLs when alerts fire.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports PineGuard's webhook-based alert logging and forward-test monitor

evidence.short_evidence: TradingView documents alerts as a core workflow for notifying users when chart conditions are met.
evidence.why_relevant: Shows that alert events are a native data stream PineGuard can collect and analyze

evidence.short_evidence: TradingView public script listings use non-repaint language as a visible selling point for indicators and strategies.
evidence.why_relevant: Shows that non-repainting is part of marketplace demand language rather than only a technical documentation issue

evidence.short_evidence: TradingView public script listings include backtest-related strategies and descriptions.
evidence.why_relevant: Shows that backtesting language appears in the public strategy marketplace

evidence.short_evidence: TradingView public script listings include webhook-related scripts and automation-oriented descriptions.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports the claim that webhook workflows are visible in the TradingView ecosystem

evidence.short_evidence: Pineify positions itself as a no-code or AI-assisted Pine Script workflow for building indicators and strategies.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports a willingness-to-pay proxy for lower-friction Pine strategy creation tools

evidence.short_evidence: Build Alpha offers a TradingView strategy generator that turns strategy ideas into Pine Script workflows.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports the existence of adjacent strategy-generation products around TradingView

evidence.short_evidence: TrendSpider markets strategy development and backtesting tools as a product feature.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports willingness-to-pay for structured strategy testing beyond manual chart inspection

evidence.short_evidence: TradersPost provides a TradingView signal workflow that connects alerts to external automation.
evidence.why_relevant: Shows that TradingView alerts already support paid downstream workflow products

evidence.short_evidence: PineConnector markets TradingView-to-MetaTrader automation based on alerts and connectors.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports the claim that alert automation is commercially relevant

evidence.short_evidence: WebhookTrade markets webhook-based automation for TradingView alerts and trading workflows.
evidence.why_relevant: Provides adjacent-product evidence for monetizing TradingView webhook data flows

evidence.short_evidence: BotJockie offers trading-system-related services that include development and review-like support.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports a weak but relevant proxy that users may pay for external strategy expertise or validation

evidence.short_evidence: Quantpedia organizes research-based trading strategy ideas and performance evidence.
evidence.why_relevant: Supports the idea that research-inspired strategy templates can be packaged as a data product

graderTour.what_to_check: Landing page states the beachhead customer and product output.
Five-step wizard: persona, strategy, available data, risks, generation.
Health score, category breakdown, and missing workflow components.
Forward-test monitor, horizon buttons, sample CSV download, payload copy.
Evidence Explorer filters by category, source, and strength.
Downloadable report as the paid data product output.
Static course deployment and production event-driven pipeline.

competitorBenchmarks.benchmark: Pineify: USD 99 lifetime; PineGen AI: USD 39-99/month
competitorBenchmarks.pineguard_implication: Supports willingness to pay for reducing Pine Script workflow friction.

competitorBenchmarks.benchmark: TradersPost: USD 41-254/month; PineConnector: USD 39-159/month; PickMyTrade: USD 50/month; CrossTrade: USD 29-99/month
competitorBenchmarks.pineguard_implication: Supports paid alert-event infrastructure and monitoring workflows.

competitorBenchmarks.benchmark: TrendSpider: USD 82-349/month; research platforms use premium access models
competitorBenchmarks.pineguard_implication: Supports paid strategy evaluation and validation products.

competitorBenchmarks.benchmark: Paid plans tied to alerts, indicators, history, and workflow capacity
competitorBenchmarks.pineguard_implication: Supports a target segment that already pays for trading workflow tooling.

productionLayers.design: User-authorized HTTPS endpoint validates payloads and acknowledges quickly.
productionLayers.why: Avoids slow synchronous processing and reduces missing-event risk.

productionLayers.design: Durable queue stores bursty alert events before analytics workers consume them.
productionLayers.why: Decouples ingestion from processing and supports retries.

productionLayers.design: Payload hashes and event IDs prevent duplicate counting.
productionLayers.why: Preserves audit quality and report integrity.

productionLayers.design: Relational metadata, raw-payload object storage, and time-series outcome tables.
productionLayers.why: Separates users, strategies, raw logs, and analytics outputs.

productionLayers.design: Daily report recomputation and near-real-time alert monitoring.
productionLayers.why: Balances reliable reporting with timely dashboard updates.

riskControls.control: Use user-authorized alert payloads and user-provided exports; avoid unauthorized scraping.
Provide workflow diagnostics only; no personalized trade recommendations or execution.
Do not collect broker passwords or trading API keys; retain only validation-relevant fields.
Start with one-time health checks, then migrate users into monitoring as alert history accumulates.
Differentiate through audit trails, evidence-backed reports, and alert/outcome history.

evidenceQuality.role: Validate that repainting, strategy testing, alerts, and webhooks are platform-level topics.
evidenceQuality.interpretation: Strong technical motivation; not direct willingness-to-pay evidence.

evidenceQuality.role: Show qualitative pain around repainting, webhook issues, and paid-script trust.
evidenceQuality.interpretation: Useful but not representative; should be described conservatively.
Benchmarks a plausible price corridor; not a direct demand curve.
Course artifact; production system needs live authorized ingestion.

scaleEconomics.architecture_implication: GitHub Pages, versioned JSON/CSV, and validation checks are sufficient.
scaleEconomics.cost_driver: Near-zero hosting cost for the academic demo.

scaleEconomics.expected_workload: Thousands of alert events per day and scheduled reports
scaleEconomics.architecture_implication: Add webhook endpoint, durable queue, PostgreSQL metadata, and daily workers.
scaleEconomics.cost_driver: Webhook ingestion, storage retention, and scheduled recomputation.

scaleEconomics.expected_workload: Millions of alert events per month across many strategies
scaleEconomics.architecture_implication: Add partitioned event tables, object storage, retries, monitoring, and cost-aware retention.
scaleEconomics.cost_driver: Event volume, market-data enrichment, retention policy, and support load.
